violations.description: Tests rely on global seeded state without explicit test-level data provisioning or dynamic data creation
violations.suggestion: Implement test-specific database seeding (e.g. creating client and report records inside beforeEach or custom page fixtures) and perform cleanup after execution.

recommendations: Adopt the Data Factories and API Setup pattern (from data-factories.md) to seed unique, isolated records per test run.
Introduce cleanup blocks or transaction resets in database triggers to prevent pollution and test interference.
summary: Excellent test-level isolation in terms of browser context: the suite uses separate, authenticated page contexts (ownerPage) for each test. The main concern is reliance on shared/global database state without test-level provisioning or cleanup discipline, which represents a medium severity isolation issue.
